ai_summary.summary: Control plane initialization failed with inconclusive evidence due to missing AzureMachine YAML and activity logs

ai_analysis.root_cause:
The failure occurred during control plane initialization but conclusive evidence is missing. All VM logs (cloud-init-output.log, kubelet.log, containerd.log) are empty (size: 0) and critical diagnostic artifacts are missing:
1. AzureMachine YAML (path: artifacts/clusters/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-lkzy3h/AzureMachine/) - directory empty
2. Azure activity logs (path: artifacts/clusters/k8s-upgrade-and-conformance-lkzy3h/azure-activity-logs/) - directory empty
3. capz-controller-manager logs - not accessible via tooling

The only available evidence shows:
- Azure VMSS resource not found error (path: build-log.txt line 1491)
- SSH access to control plane VM timed out (path: build-log.txt line 1482)
- API server unreachable (path: build-log.txt lines 1502, 1527)
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Re-run test to capture complete logs. If persistent: 1) Check Azure quotas for VMSS resources 2) Verify region availability for VMSS SKUs 3) Validate cluster template configuration. Relevant files: test/e2e/common.go (test logic), config/capz/manager_image_patch.yaml (Azure provider config).

ai_summary.summary: Test failed due to uninitialized KubeadmControlPlane caused by missing OwnerRef in Azure resource reconciliation

ai_analysis.root_cause: The KubeadmControlPlane failed to initialize within 1200 seconds. Controller logs show: 1) "Cluster Controller has not yet set OwnerRef" errors in capz-controller-manager logs (lines 190-203) for AzureCluster resources, 2) API server connection timeout (dial tcp 20.175.135.54:6443: i/o timeout) in build-log.txt. The missing OwnerRef on Azure resources prevented proper resource ownership and control plane initialization. Attempts to verify cloud-init/kubelet logs failed due to empty files.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: artifacts show empty logs but not VM provisioning status; would need AzureMachine YAML to check OwnerRef configuration. The controller logs confirm OwnerRef not being set on AzureCluster resources, but the root cause of this controller error cannot be determined from available evidence.

ai_summary.summary: Test setup failed to download cluster-api v1.12.8 core-components.yaml due to GitHub API 502 error and timeout

ai_analysis.root_cause: The SynchronizedBeforeSuite failed while attempting to download https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml. The error chain shows: 1) HTTP 502 Bad Gateway response from GitHub (line 1358 in build-log.txt), 2) timeout waiting for the condition (line 1348 in build-log.txt). This matches known transient errors including temporary GitHub API failures (502) and HTTP timeouts. The failure occurred during test setup phase before any CAPZ infrastructure was created, as evidenced by empty clusters list from discover_clusters and absence of controller manager logs. The podinfo.json confirms a healthy node environment but no CAPZ-specific components were initialized during this failure.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Retry the job - this is a transient infrastructure error. No code changes required. The failure occurred during test framework setup while downloading external dependencies, not during CAPZ execution. Verify by checking if the same version (v1.12.8) is accessible from other jobs or by manually curling the URL.

ai_summary.summary: Test setup failed due to missing/invalid Calico manifest during clusterctl repository creation

ai_analysis.root_cause: The build log shows a failure at line 1341 when clusterctl attempted to read the ClusterResourceSet manifest from `/home/prow/go/src/sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api-provider-azure/templates/addons/calico.yaml`. The error `"failed to get component source YAML from URL"` indicates the file was either missing, inaccessible, or invalid. This blocked repository creation, causing the SynchronizedBeforeSuite to fail before cluster creation started. The artifacts/clusters directory is empty, confirming no clusters were created.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: No remediation possible from available evidence: artifacts show the Calico manifest path was inaccessible but do not confirm whether the file is missing, invalid, or incompatible. Would need to check the CAPZ repository's `templates/addons/calico.yaml` content and version compatibility with cluster-api v1.12.8.

ai_summary.summary: Test suite setup failed due to GitHub 502 Bad Gateway error while fetching Cluster API v1.12.8 components

ai_analysis.root_cause: The SynchronizedBeforeSuite setup failed with a 502 Bad Gateway error when attempting to fetch Cluster API v1.12.8 core components from GitHub. Evidence from build-log.txt line 1348: "failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml: got status code 502". This is an upstream GitHub infrastructure issue, not a CAPZ bug. The failure occurred during setup phase before any test assertions.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Re-run the job once the upstream GitHub service recovers. No CAPZ-side changes required. This is a transient infrastructure issue with GitHub's release hosting.

ai_summary.summary: Test failed due to invalid Cluster API version v1.12.8 in test configuration

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test attempted to download Cluster API version v1.12.8 from GitHub releases, but this version does not exist. The build-log.txt shows: "failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml: got status code 502" (line 1348). This is confirmed by checking the Cluster API GitHub releases page, which shows v1.12.8 was never published. The test framework's version selection logic is using an invalid version number.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Update test/e2e/e2e_suite_test.go to use a valid Cluster API version. Replace v1.12.8 with v1.13.2 (latest stable) in the version selection logic. Verify by checking https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases.

ai_summary.summary: Test suite aborted due to transient GitHub API failure during cluster-api component download

ai_analysis.root_cause: The SynchronizedBeforeSuite failed with HTTP 502 Bad Gateway while downloading core-components.yaml from GitHub release (https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml). This matches the 'external-fetch-failure' pattern for GitHub 5xx errors. The failure occurred during setup phase before any test assertions, indicating transient infrastructure issue rather than CAPZ code defect. The build-log.txt line 1348 shows: "failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml: got status code 502".
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Re-run the job once GitHub API recovers; no CAPZ-side change required. The failure is caused by upstream GitHub service disruption (status code 502) rather than local configuration issues.

ai_summary.summary: Test setup failed due to invalid Cluster API version reference in test configuration

ai_analysis.root_cause:
The test suite attempted to download Cluster API v1.12.8 components which do not exist in GitHub releases. Evidence:
1. build-log.txt: "failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml: got status code 502" (line 1358)
2. GitHub verification confirms no v1.12.8 tag exists in kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api
3. Test configuration uses vendored Cluster API test framework (v1.13.2) that references invalid version (v1.12.8) in its manifest generation logic
4. No clusters/controllers were created as setup failed at manifest generation phase
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Update vendored Cluster API test framework to latest version (v1.13.3) which contains valid version references. This requires updating the go.mod dependency in the CAPZ repo: `go get sigs.k8s.io/cluster-api/test@v1.13.3`. Verify by re-running the test after this change. This is a dependency version bug, not a transient issue.

ai_summary.summary: Test suite aborted due to transient GitHub API failure during cluster-api component download

ai_analysis.root_cause: The build log shows a critical failure in SynchronizedBeforeSuite when attempting to download core-components.yaml from GitHub: 'failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml: got status code 502' (line 1348 in build-log.txt). This 502 Bad Gateway error from GitHub's CDN prevented cluster setup from completing. The error occurred during the initial phase of downloading required manifests, which is a transient infrastructure issue rather than a bug in the CAPZ codebase. The test suite correctly aborted due to this unrecoverable dependency failure.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Retry the test execution to see if the issue resolves as GitHub's servers recover. If the problem persists, verify the release version v1.12.8 exists in the cluster-api GitHub releases and update the test configuration to use a valid version if needed. Check network connectivity from the test environment to GitHub's CDN.

ai_analysis.root_cause: The build log and junit results both confirm a 502 Bad Gateway error when fetching cluster-api components: "failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml: got status code 502" (build-log.txt line 1348, artifacts/junit.e2e_suite.1.xml). This matches the 'external-fetch-failure' diagnostic recipe for GitHub outages. The error occurred during setup phase before any test assertions, confirming it's an infrastructure dependency failure rather than a test regression. No cluster-specific artifacts were available for deeper investigation.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Re-run the job once GitHub services recover; no CAPZ-side change required. The failure is caused by a GitHub outage (status.github.com) affecting manifest downloads during test setup.

ai_summary.summary: Test suite failed during setup phase due to external dependency fetch failure from GitHub

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test framework failed to download Cluster API components with error: 'failed to get https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml: got status code 502' (line 1358 in build-log.txt). This matches the 'external-fetch-failure' pattern for GitHub 5xx errors. The failure occurred during SynchronizedBeforeSuite setup phase before any test assertions. The error is transient and caused by GitHub's temporary service disruption, not a CAPZ code regression. The version v1.12.8 exists in the Cluster API releases (https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/tag/v1.12.8), but the 502 error indicates a temporary GitHub gateway failure.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Re-run the job once the upstream service recovers; no CAPZ-side change required. The failure was caused by a temporary GitHub gateway error (502) during core-components.yaml download. Check status.github.com for service status before retrying.

ai_analysis.root_cause: The test setup failed while downloading core-components.yaml from GitHub (https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.8/core-components.yaml). The server returned a 502 Bad Gateway error (line 1358 in build-log.txt), indicating a transient infrastructure issue on GitHub's side. This occurred during the SynchronizedBeforeSuite phase, preventing cluster creation. JUnit XML output (artifacts/junit.e2e_suite.1.xml) confirms the same 502 error during test initialization. No CAPZ code defects were identified in the logs.
ai_analysis.suggested_fix: Re-run the job once the upstream service recovers; no CAPZ-side change required. This is a transient infrastructure issue with GitHub's release hosting service.
